meta.caveat: State-level base rate on vehicle purchases only. Many states add county/city/district taxes (often 1-5%). Rates prefill the calculator but stay fully editable - buyers should verify with their state DMV/DOR.
sources: Florida Dept. of Revenue TIP 26A01-01, 'Motor Vehicle Sales Tax Rates by State' (Feb 11, 2026)
Individual state DOR/DMV published vehicle tax rates (2025-2026)
NerdWallet, 'Does a Trade-In Reduce Sales Tax?' (trade-in credit rules)

states.name: Alabama
states.note: 2% state automotive rate; local taxes add more

states.name: Alaska
states.note: No state sales tax; some localities levy their own

states.name: California
states.note: Tax applies to full price before trade-in

states.name: Connecticut
states.note: 7.75% on vehicles over $50,000

states.name: Delaware
states.note: No sales tax; 4.25% doc fee on purchase price applies instead

states.name: District of Columbia
states.note: Excise 6-8%+ tiered by weight/MPG; no trade-in deduction

states.name: Georgia
states.note: One-time TAVT (title tax) instead of sales tax; trade-in reduces taxable value

states.name: Hawaii
states.note: GET ~4-4.5% with county surcharge; full price taxed

states.name: Iowa
states.note: 5% one-time registration fee in lieu of sales tax

states.name: Kentucky
states.note: 6% motor vehicle usage tax; trade-in credit rules differ for new vs used - verify

states.name: Louisiana
states.note: State rate 5% since 2025; parish taxes add more

states.name: Maryland
states.note: 6% titling excise tax; dealer trade-in allowance deductible

states.name: Michigan
states.note: Trade-in credit capped (phasing to full credit by 2029)

states.name: Mississippi
states.note: 5% on vehicles (lower than general rate)

states.name: Montana
states.note: No sales tax

states.name: Nevada
states.note: Local taxes push combined rate to 8%+ in Clark County

states.name: New Hampshire
states.note: No sales tax

states.name: New Mexico
states.note: 4% motor vehicle excise tax

states.name: New York
states.note: Local taxes typically add ~4%

states.name: North Carolina
states.note: 3% Highway Use Tax instead of sales tax

states.name: North Dakota
states.note: 5% motor vehicle excise tax

states.name: Oklahoma
states.note: 3.25% excise; additional vehicle sales tax/local may apply - verify

states.name: Oregon
states.note: No sales tax; 0.5% dealer privilege tax on new vehicles

states.name: Pennsylvania
states.note: +1-2% in Allegheny County / Philadelphia

states.name: South Carolina
states.note: Infrastructure Maintenance Fee capped at $500 total

states.name: South Dakota
states.note: 4% motor vehicle excise tax

states.name: Tennessee
states.note: Local single-article tax adds up to ~$44-160

states.name: Utah
states.note: Local taxes add more

states.name: Virginia
states.note: 4.15% SUT (min $75) on full sale price; no trade-in deduction

states.name: Washington
states.note: 6.5% + 0.3% motor vehicle tax; local taxes add more
